name: Fluoroscopy Upper GI
description: Fluoroscopic examination of the upper gastrointestinal tract

findings.technique: Fluoroscopic examination of the upper GI tract was performed with barium contrast.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Esophagus
sections.content: The esophagus demonstrates [normal caliber / no narrowing / no ulceration / no mass]. The motility is [normal / no dysmotility]. No reflux identified.

sections.name: GE Junction
sections.content: The gastroesophageal junction is [competent / no hiatal hernia / no reflux]. The Z-line is [at normal position / not patulous].

sections.name: Stomach
sections.content: The stomach demonstrates [normal distensibility / no filling defect / no ulcer / no mass]. The gastric folds are [normal / not thickened].

sections.name: Pylorus
sections.content: The pylorus is [patent / opens normally / no obstruction]. No pyloric stenosis.

sections.name: Duodenum
sections.content: The duodenum (C-loop and D-loop) is [normal / no abnormality]. The ampulla of Vater is [visualized / normal].

impression:
1. Normal fluoroscopic upper GI examination.
2. No abnormality of the esophagus, stomach, or duodenum.
author: RadReport Templates
